User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user profile within the Code Ascent application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

username.description: The user's chosen username, must be unique.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification. (Relationship: User 1:1 AuthenticationSystem)

currentLearningPathId.description: Reference to the User's currently active LearningPath. (Relationship: LearningPath 1:N User)

lastActive.description: Timestamp of the user's last activity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile image.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred programming language for learning, e.g., 'Python', 'JavaScript'.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents a high-level educational course, consisting of multiple modules, e.g., 'Introduction to Python'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

description.description: A brief description of what the course covers.

difficultyLevel.description: The general difficulty level of the course, e.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Expert'.

programmingLanguage.description: The primary programming language taught in this course.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the course.

Module.title: Module
Module.description: Represents a distinct section or unit within a Course, containing multiple exercises.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Module entity.

description.description: A description of the module's content.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this module belongs to. (Relationship: Course 1:N Module)

orderIndex.description: The sequential order of this module within its parent course.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a specific programming concept, technique, or ability tracked in the visual skill tree.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

name.description: The name of the skill, e.g., 'Variables', 'Loops', 'Function Definition'.

description.description: A detailed description of what this skill entails.

parentSkillIds.description: References to parent skills in the skill tree, indicating prerequisites or broader categories. (Relationship: Skill N:N Skill)

programmingLanguage.description: The programming language to which this skill primarily applies (optional, for general concepts).

Exercise.title: Exercise
Exercise.description: Represents an interactive coding exercise within a module.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Exercise entity.

description.description: The problem statement or instructions for the exercise.

initialCode.description: The starter code provided to the user for this exercise.

testCases.description: A serialized representation of test cases to validate user solutions.

moduleId.description: Reference to the Module this exercise belongs to. (Relationship: Module 1:N Exercise)

skillIds.description: References to Skills that this exercise helps to develop or assess. (Relationship: Skill N:N Exercise)

difficultyRating.description: A rating of the exercise's difficulty (e.g., 1-5).

AIChallenge.title: AIChallenge
AIChallenge.description: Represents a dynamically generated coding challenge tailored to a user's weaknesses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIChallenge entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User for whom this challenge was generated. (Relationship: User 1:N AIChallenge)

generatedDateTime.description: Timestamp when the AI challenge was generated.

title.description: A title for the AI-generated challenge.

problemDescription.description: The full problem description for the AI-generated challenge.

initialCode.description: Optional starter code provided for the AI-generated challenge.

targetSkillIds.description: References to the specific Skills this challenge aims to improve. (Relationship: Skill N:N AIChallenge)

difficultyLevel.description: The perceived difficulty level of the AI-generated challenge.

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the user has completed this AI-generated challenge.

completionDateTime.description: Timestamp when the AI challenge was completed.

LearningPath.title: LearningPath
LearningPath.description: Represents a dynamically adapted sequence of modules or courses for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LearningPath entity.

name.description: A name for the learning path, e.g., 'Python Novice to Intermediate'.

description.description: A description of the learning path's objectives.

moduleIds.description: An ordered list of Module IDs that comprise this learning path. (Relationship: Module N:N LearningPath)

UserSkillProgress.title: UserSkillProgress
UserSkillProgress.description: Tracks a user's mastery level for a specific skill, contributing to the visual skill tree.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserSkillProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User whose skill progress is being tracked. (Relationship: User 1:N UserSkillProgress)

skillId.description: Reference to the Skill being tracked. (Relationship: Skill 1:N UserSkillProgress)

masteryLevel.description: The current mastery level of the user for this skill (e.g., 0-100 or 0-5).

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp when the mastery level for this skill was last updated.

UserExerciseAttempt.title: UserExerciseAttempt
UserExerciseAttempt.description: Records a user's attempt at an Exercise or an AIChallenge, including their code and AI feedback.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserExerciseAttempt entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the attempt. (Relationship: User 1:N UserExerciseAttempt)

referencedEntityId.description: The ID of the entity (Exercise or AIChallenge) that was attempted. (Relationship: Exercise 1:N UserExerciseAttempt OR AIChallenge 1:N UserExerciseAttempt)

referencedEntityType.description: The type of entity that was attempted, either 'Exercise' or 'AIChallenge'.

submittedCode.description: The code submitted by the user for this attempt.

submissionDateTime.description: Timestamp when the code was submitted.

isCorrect.description: Indicates whether the submitted code passed all tests/was deemed correct.

score.description: A numerical score for the attempt, if applicable (e.g., 0-100).

aiFeedback.description: The personalized AI feedback generated for this specific code submission.

timeSpentSeconds.description: The duration, in seconds, the user spent on this attempt before submission.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the owner matching `request.auth.uid`. The `userId` in the path ensures authorization independence for the user's own profile data.
Stores general course information. Designed for public read access by all authenticated users. No specific authorization fields are denormalized for read access, as it's globally accessible content.

Stores modules belonging to a specific course. Designed for public read access by all authenticated users. Includes denormalized 'courseId' field to facilitate queries and provide context without parent document lookups.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent course.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the module.

definition.description: Stores definitions of programming skills. Designed for public read access by all authenticated users as part of the global skill tree.

Stores interactive coding exercises. Designed for public read access by all authenticated users. Includes denormalized 'moduleId' and 'courseId' fields for query flexibility and contextual awareness without needing parent document lookups.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent course.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent module.

params.name: exerciseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the exercise.

definition.description: Stores dynamically generated AI challenges, personalized for each user. Access is restricted to the owner. The 'userId' in the path serves as the denormalized authorization context, enabling atomic operations and secure `list` queries.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this AI challenge.

params.name: aiChallengeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the AI challenge.

definition.description: Stores dynamically adapted learning paths for each user. Access is restricted to the owner. The 'userId' in the path serves as the denormalized authorization context, enabling atomic operations and secure `list` queries.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this learning path.

params.name: learningPathId
params.description: The unique identifier of the learning path.

definition.description: Tracks a user's mastery level for a specific skill. Access is restricted to the owner. The 'userId' in the path serves as the denormalized authorization context, enabling atomic operations and secure `list` queries. The 'skillId' is used as the document ID for efficient lookup.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user whose skill progress is being tracked.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique identifier of the skill being tracked, also used as the document ID.

definition.description: Records a user's attempt at an exercise or AI challenge. Access is restricted to the owner. The 'userId' in the path serves as the denormalized authorization context, enabling atomic operations and secure `list` queries.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who made the attempt.

params.name: attemptId
params.description: The unique identifier of the exercise attempt.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to define administrative users. The existence of a document at this path for a given `uid` grants admin privileges. This uses the 'Existence over Content' principle for authorization independence. (Note: Entity schema refers to User as no specific AdminRole schema was provided, but it would typically be a minimal document indicating an admin role).

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by adhering strictly to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & Denormalization:**
*   **User-Specific Data:** For all entities directly owned by a user (User profile, AI Challenges, Learning Paths, User Skill Progress, User Exercise Attempts), the `userId` is embedded directly into the document path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/aiChallenges/{aiChallengeId}`). This ensures that security rules can always directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `userId` in the path, eliminating the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to parent documents to determine ownership. This approach allows for atomic creation and modification of user-owned data.
*   **Public Content (Courses, Modules, Exercises, Skills):** These entities are designed for public read access by any authenticated user. Since read access is universal for authenticated users, no complex hierarchical authorization logic is required for reading, thus avoiding `get()` calls on parent documents. For write access (e.g., by administrators), a separate 'roles' collection (e.g., `/roles_admin/{uid}`) would be used, relying on 'existence over content' for authorization independence.
*   **Denormalization:** The `Module` entity within the `/courses/{courseId}/modules/{moduleId}` path will contain a `courseId` field, and the `Exercise` entity within `/courses/{courseId}/modules/{moduleId}/exercises/{exerciseId}` will contain both `moduleId` and `courseId` fields. While the path provides the hierarchy, these denormalized fields are critical for efficient querying (e.g., 'give me all exercises in Course X' without needing to traverse modules) and ensure that an individual document has sufficient context without requiring `get()` operations, especially if granular, non-public read access were ever considered.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   **Structural Segregation:** By clearly separating public content (`/courses`, `/skills`) from private user data (`/users/{userId}/...`), we enable straightforward security rules. Public content collections can have simple `allow read: if request.auth != null;` rules. 
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** For all user-specific data, the `userId` in the path (`/users/{userId}/...`) ensures that `list` operations are implicitly secured. A user can only list documents within their own `/users/{userId}/` subcollections because the rule `allow read: if request.auth.uid == userId;` will naturally filter results to only what the authenticated user owns, fulfilling the QAP principle without filtering data on the server side.
*   **Consistent Access Patterns:** The standardized use of path-based ownership and distinct collections for content vs. user data makes authorization intent explicit and simplifies the writing and debugging of security rules.
